structural_claim: Local properties combined with global topological constraints create extraordinary rigidity that reduces apparent complexity to canonical forms

supporting_results.structural_conclusion: Local holomorphicity combined with global simple connectivity reduces all planar domains to the single canonical form of the unit disk
Local analyticity combined with global boundedness forces functions to be constant, showing maximal rigidity
Local multiplicative properties of integers combined with global well-ordering reduce all integers to unique canonical prime factorizations
Local quadratic properties combined with global prime structure create rigid reciprocal symmetries that constrain solvability patterns
